_meta.scope: steps 0-7 (auth -> capabilities -> params-to-commands -> param-defaults -> ucp-review -> params-to-capabilities)
_meta.note: All auth/param decisions made autonomously per connectus-migration-SKILL.md. Low-confidence calls flagged with 'confidence' and 'flags'. set-auth forces interpolated:true on every profile.

auth.name: EdgeGrid
auth.reason: Akamai EdgeGrid signing uses 3 co-equal tokens (clientToken+accessToken+clientSecret) via EdgeGridAuth at Akamai_WAF.py:7447. No dominant canonical role -> Passthrough per skill 1.2.2a. interpolated:true forced by set-auth.
auth.notes: Old flat type:4 params (clientToken/accessToken/clientSecret) are hidden:true -> excluded; only the visible type:9 credentials .password leaves mapped (hiddenusername:true suppresses .identifier).

ucp_param_default_review.fix: Akamai_WAF.py:7437 proxy = params.get("proxy", False) (behavior-preserving; BaseClient consumes proxy truthily)

auth.name: EdgeGrid
auth.reason: EdgeGrid 3-token signing (clienttoken/accesstoken/clientsecret _creds) via EdgeGridAuth at Akamai_SIEM.py:1235. Passthrough per 1.2.2a (3 co-equal tokens). interpolated forced.
auth.notes: Flat type:4 token params hidden:true -> excluded. configIds (required, no default, connection-routing) rejected as a code default and moved to other_connection per branch-b reject rule.

Akamai WAF SIEM.params_to_commands_note: Static analyzer returned empty per-command lists; fetch behavioral params (fetchTime,fetchLimit) added by source review per err-on-inclusion. Hidden:[xsoar] params (page_size,beta_page_size,max_concurrent_tasks,isFetchEvents,should_skip_decode_events,eventFetchInterval) excluded.

fixes: fetch_incidents_command call: fetchTime default 1 hours, fetchLimit default 20
remaining_uncertain_justified: <dynamic> splat @100 = local request params not config

flags: RE-RUN per updated hidden rule: params hidden:[xsoar] (page_size, beta_page_size, max_concurrent_tasks, should_skip_decode_events, eventFetchInterval) are NOT platform-hidden so now carried through into Params to Commands + Params to Capabilities (Log Collection). isFetchEvents/longRunning stay framework.

justified: first_fetch defaulted via if/else DEFAULT_FIRST_FETCH at line103

auth.name: API Key
auth.reason: API key sent as ?key=<api_key> query param (GoogleSafeBrowsingV2.py:217). api_creds.password=API key, api_creds.identifier=client_id (sent as clientId in request body). Two leaves (key + client_id); client_id is non-canonical role so classified Passthrough rather than APIKey (which only allows role 'key').
auth.notes: Flat api_key/client_id hidden:true excluded.
auth.confidence_reason: client_id role placement (in profile vs other_connection) is a judgment call; kept in profile because it is part of the auth/client identity sent with each request.

auth.name: Basic Auth

ucp_param_default_review.new_pattern: multi-select (type:16) param with YML default read bare then used with 'in'/'join' breaks under UCP when absent (None) -> 'All' in None raises TypeError. Fix: params.get(x) or [default-list].
justified: isFetch passed to test_module is None-safe boolean

flags: connector_id='Akamai' in CSV - unexpected for GuardiCore; flagged for review (GuardiCore is Akamai-owned so may be intentional).

auth.name: Client Credentials
auth.reason: OAuth2 client-credentials style: API3 client_id+client_secret POSTed to /login returning access_token (Looker.py:95-101). No user code -> Passthrough. credentials_api3_client.identifier=client_id, .password=client_secret.
auth.notes: Flat client_secret type4 hidden:true excluded. Flat client_id type0 is VISIBLE+required (legacy alt path) but redundant with credentials_api3_client.identifier; mapped the widget leaves only to avoid double-mapping.
auth.confidence_reason: flat visible client_id not placed anywhere (widget identifier covers it); a reviewer may prefer adding flat client_id to other_connection.

flags: RE-RUN: flat visible required client_id added to other_connection (resolves step-9 handler coverage gap). credentials_api3_client widget still carries the auth secret.

auth.name: Basic Auth

Clarizen IAM.params_to_commands_note: IAMApiModule integration; static analyzer returned empty per-command lists (params consumed inside IAMCommand). IAM dispatch config params (create/update/enable/disable toggles, create_if_not_exists, mapper_in) attributed to the relevant iam-* commands per err-on-inclusion. mapper_out excluded (platform-hidden).

justified: 7 uncertain params (create/update/enable/disable toggles, create_if_not_exists, mapper_in/out) read via params.get() (None-safe) and passed to IAMCommand as truthy flags / mapper names; cross-function flow only, no strict converter -> safe (ClarizenIAM.py:310-333)

flags: IAMApiModule integration; connector_id=Plainview per CSV (Clarizen is a Planview/Plainview product).

auth.reason: Pure local classifier - config has only reliability+reputation (type15 score params), no credentials, no HTTP client (Cryptocurrency.py main reads only those two). auth_types=[] (NoneRequired).

fixes: Cryptocurrency.py:74-75 reliability=params.get("reliability") or "B - Usually reliable"; reputation=params.get("reputation") or "Suspicious" (were bare params["x"] subscripts -> KeyError under UCP). Defaults taken from YML defaultvalue.

API Key (api_key.password, type9 hiddenusername:true) used as Bearer (:142); credentials username/password posted to login endpoint (:221-222). Sorted (type,name): APIKey before Plain.

justified: argToBoolean(params.get(unsecure,False)) and proxy reads have defaults (FortiGate.py:5791-5792); arg_to_number calls operate on args.

auth.name: Account Credentials

auth.notes: The credentials widget can also carry a vault-attached SSH private key (CREDENTIALS.get(credentials).get(sshkey), :22-23) for key-pair auth; the two standard leaves are mapped, key-pair rides inside the same Passthrough secret bag (vault-sourced). warehouse/database/schema/role are per-command behavioral defaults (also command args) -> Params to Commands.

auth.name: API Key
auth.reason: Single API key (credentials_api_key type9 hiddenusername:true) sent as X-API-KEY header (WhatIsMyBrowser.py:29). Legacy flat api_key type4 hidden:true excluded. -> APIKey, role key.

fixes: WhatIsMyBrowser.py:18 PROXY = demisto.params().get("proxy", False) (was bare .get without default; checkbox silent-behavior-change flag). Behavior-preserving; PROXY var is actually unused (handle_proxy handles proxy).

auth.reason: configuration:[] - no params at all. Shells out to local dnstwist binary, no credentials/remote auth. auth_types=[] (NoneRequired).

justified: no configuration params; test-module runs a local subprocess (dnstwist -h). limit int() is on a command arg with YML default.

auth.name: Basic Auth
auth.reason: credentials type9 (both leaves) posted as grant_type=password username/password to mint a token (TrustwaveSEG.py:64-69). Username/password login -> Plain.

trustwave secure email gateway.params_to_commands_note: all 14 trustwave-seg-* commands use command args only; tm-only.

justified: host/config_port/api_port uncertain are connection params (in other_connection, YML defaults localhost/19007/19006) used as strings in URLs/ports - not strict converters at read site; injected by connectus connection layer.

flags: connector_id=LevelBlue per CSV (Trustwave is now LevelBlue/AT&T Cybersecurity).

auth.name: WinRM Credentials
auth.reason: credentials type9 (both leaves) -> PSCredential for WinRM remote session with selectable Authentication method (PowerShellRemoting.ps1:20-21,842). user+password WinRM login -> Plain.
auth.notes: auth_method is the auth-scheme selector (Negotiate default) -> connection-adjacent.

PowerShell Remoting.params_to_commands_note: PowerShell integration; 7 ps-remote-* commands use command args only.
flags: powershell beta integration

auth.name: Bot Token
auth.reason: Single bot token (credentials_api_token type9 hiddenusername:true) embedded in URL path https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/ (Telegram.py:16-17). Legacy flat token type4 hidden:true excluded. Single static token -> APIKey, role key.

justified: token reads use .get chains; no strict converter on config params; main() raises explicit DemistoException if token missing.

flags: beta integration

auth.name: WinRM Credentials
auth.reason: credentials type9 (both leaves) used as winrm.Session auth=(username,password) with NTLM transport (WindowsRemoteManagement.py:28,31). user+password WinRM login -> Plain.
auth.notes: auth_type (type15 select, only ntlm) is the auth-scheme selector -> connection-adjacent. decode is a per-command-overridable behavioral config -> Params to Commands (excluded from other_connection).

Windows Remote Management.params_to_commands_note: analyzer empty; instance decode is the per-command fallback (args.get(decode, client.decode)) for both run commands -> attributed by source review.

justified: auth_type/default_host/realm uncertain are connection params (other_connection) read with .get / string-compared (auth_type==ntlm) / None-guarded; auth_type has YML default ntlm; not strict converters.

flags: beta integration
